---
title: Research Protocol
subtitle: CITF Emails Analysis
description: A planning document outlining the project's scope, methods and projected outcomes.
date-modified: 2025-08-11
date-format: long
---

::: {.callout-note}
Note that the project has significantly evolved since drafting this protocol, and this document does not reflect our current scope of work. It still has value as a marker of our original intent.
:::

# Overview and Scope


## Predictions and Expectations


# Materials and Methods


## The corpus of works


# Analysis


# Methodological notes and ideas


# Outcomes


# Logistics
## Workflows
The is a [quarto](https://quarto.org/)-based project situated in a git repository ([CITF-emails](https://github.com/zackbatist/CITF-emails)).
Zack is the repository's primary maintainer, but Isak has been granted full administrative access, including the ability to make commits directly to the repository without submitting a pull request.
Zack and Isak will clearly communicate about what they are working on so as to avoid generating conflicting commit histories which would produce a messy merge scenario.

For less technical users, it would be best to annotate automatically-generated PDFs or to work with an un-tracked copy of the markdown files, whose diffs can then be identified and applied by someone who is more familiar with using git.

Here are a few procedural notes and workflows:

- All writing is done in the file pertaining to the specific section, which are included in their corresponding manuscript file (e.g., `topic-modelling.qmd`), which is also where the paper's main parameters are specified.
- References are cited using `@` followed by the citekey, which follows a strict pattern of the first author's surname followed by the year of publication, all lowercase and without spaces, e.g. `@batist2024`.
- References are stored in `assets/emails-analysis.bib` which is an automatically-updated export of Zack's Zotero library. If you want to include a reference that is not yet in the library, refer to the DOI and Zack will add it in his revisions.
- Although we are keeping this as a private repository for now, all of this can be made into a public website. To preview the site locally, use `quarto preview` and navigate to `localhost:7777` in your browser.

Here is an overview of the directory structure:

[TBD]{.mark}

## Timeline
[TBD]{.mark}

# References
::: {#refs}
:::
